pattern: Sorting candidates lets a depth-first search reuse the current value (recurse from the same index) and stop a branch once the running value exceeds the target, so each multiset is generated once and in non-decreasing order.
hints: Each candidate may be picked any number of times, so the choice at every step is either to use the current candidate again or to move on to the next one.
Sort the candidates and search with a start index: never look back before it, which forces every combination to come out in non-decreasing order and prevents reorderings of the same multiset.
Recurse from index i (not i+1) after adding candidates[i] so it can repeat, subtract it from the remaining target, and break out of the loop as soon as candidates[i] exceeds what is left.

edge_inputs.description: small distinct set, one value reused and one taken alone
several combinations including a long all-twos one
single candidate larger than the target yields nothing
one candidate divides the target exactly, reused repeatedly
the smallest allowed candidate value reused to reach a small target
candidates each exceed the target so no combination exists

stress.description: small distinct candidate set with a small target so the exponential brute oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(5, size)))
    cand = rng.sample(range(2, 11), n)
    target = rng.randint(2, 14)
    return [cand, target]
stress.note: candidate values and target are kept small because the oracle enumerates multisets
